card.intuition: Randomly zero out each neuron's activation with probability p during training. This prevents co-adaptation: neurons cannot rely on specific partners being active, so each must learn robust features. At inference, disable dropout and scale activations by (1−p) — or use inverted dropout (scale by 1/(1−p) during training, no scaling at inference).
card.analogy: Team training with random absences: if teammates randomly miss practice, each player must learn to perform independently rather than relying on one star player. The resulting team plays more robustly under real-game conditions when everyone shows up.
card.time_complexity: O(d) per layer — elementwise mask application
when_to_use: Large networks where overfitting is a concern
Applied after fully-connected layers; less common in conv layers (use spatial dropout)
p=0.5 for hidden layers; p=0.2 for input layers — tune as a hyperparameter
gotchas: Disable during inference (model.eval()) — or predictions will be stochastic and wrong
Inverted dropout is preferred: scale at train time so inference code stays unchanged
Dropout and batch norm interact poorly — avoid using both in the same layer

exercise.starter_code:
import random

def dropout(x, p, training=True):
    """
    Inverted dropout: during training, zero each element with probability p
    and scale survivors by 1/(1-p) so expected value is unchanged.
    During inference, return x unchanged.
    x: list of floats
    p: drop probability (0 <= p < 1)
    """
    if not training or p == 0.0:
        return x
    scale = ___________
    return [xi * scale if random.random() > p else 0.0 for xi in x]
hints: Inverted dropout scales surviving activations by 1/(1−p) so the expected value equals the original. This way, inference code needs no modification.
exercise.test_code:
import random
random.seed(42)

# Inference mode: no dropout
x = [1.0, 2.0, 3.0, 4.0]
assert dropout(x, p=0.5, training=False) == x

# Training mode: expected value preserved on average
random.seed(0)
total = [0.0] * 4
N = 5000
for _ in range(N):
    out = dropout(x, p=0.5, training=True)
    for i in range(4): total[i] += out[i]
avg = [t/N for t in total]
for i in range(4):
    assert abs(avg[i] - x[i]) < 0.15, f'E[dropout] != original at index {i}: {avg[i]:.3f} vs {x[i]}'

# p=0: no dropout
assert dropout(x, p=0.0) == x
print('PASS')
